2	  "body": "All terms introduced in the recently merged PR #3445 (merged despite a failure of the CI check, please don’t do that…) violate taxon constraints.

This is because all those terms have the same following two assertions:

> '[in taxon](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002162)' some [Astacidea](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/NCBITaxon_6712)
> '[in taxon](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002162)' some [Brachyura](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/NCBITaxon_6752)

This is likely a misunderstanding of the meaning of the '[in taxon](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002162)' relation. “X '[in taxon](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002162)' some Y” means that **X is specific to the Y taxon**, i.e. X cannot exist in any other taxon that is not Y or a descendant of Y. From that meaning, it follows that a term should never have more than _one_ '[in taxon](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002162)' assertion; once a term has been asserted to be '[in taxon](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002162)' some Y1, any other assertion '[in taxon](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002162)' some Y2 will cause a contradiction, unless Y2 happens to be exactly equivalent to Y1.

If you want to state that an anatomical entity is “known to exist in a given taxon“ but without implying anything about its existence in other taxa, what you need is '[present in taxon](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002175)'.

If you want to state that an anatomical entity exists in a handful of taxa _T1_, _T2_, ... _Tn_, exclusive of all other taxa, then '[in taxon](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002162)' is the correct relation but you need to find the closest parental taxon that encompasses all the _T1_, _T2_, ... _Tn_ taxa. In the present case, [Astacidea](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/NCBITaxon_6712) and [Brachyura](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/NCBITaxon_6752) are both subtaxa of [Pleocyemata](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/NCBITaxon_6692), so the correct way to state that an anatomical entity only exists in [Astacidea](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/NCBITaxon_6712) and [Brachyura](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/NCBITaxon_6752) is

> '[in taxon](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002162)' some [Pleocyemata](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/NCBITaxon_6692)

---
Uberon contributors: Please ensure you read and understand the [guide to taxon constraints](https://oboacademy.github.io/obook/explanation/taxon-constraints-explainer) before adding/editing assertions that involve taxon constraints (any assertion that uses one of '[in taxon](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002162)', '[never in taxon](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002161)', [present in taxon](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002175)').

Uberon reviewers: Please do not merge a PR if the CI check failed -- at least not without asking a member of the “tech team“ first. It can happen that sometimes a CI failure is caused by “external“ factors (e.g. network issues), and in that case _and in that case only_ it may be fine to proceed with merging the PR despite the failure. But do not merge a PR with a failed check unless you know for sure it is an instance of an external problem.",
3	  "comments": [],
4	  "title": "Newly introduced crab and lobster terms violate taxon constraints",
5	  "url": "https://github.com/obophenotype/uberon/issues/3454"
6	}

> '[in taxon](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002162)' some [Brachyura](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/NCBITaxon_6752)

This is likely a misunderstanding of the meaning of the '[in taxon](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002162)' relation. “X '[in taxon](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002162)' some Y” means that **X is specific to the Y taxon**, i.e. X cannot exist in any other taxon that is not Y or a descendant of Y. From that meaning, it follows that a term should never have more than _one_ '[in taxon](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002162)' assertion; once a term has been asserted to be '[in taxon](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002162)' some Y1, any other assertion '[in taxon](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002162)' some Y2 will cause a contradiction, unless Y2 happens to be exactly equivalent to Y1.

If you want to state that an anatomical entity is “known to exist in a given taxon“ but without implying anything about its existence in other taxa, what you need is '[present in taxon](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002175)'.

In the present case, [Astacidea](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/NCBITaxon_6712) and [Brachyura](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/NCBITaxon_6752) are both subtaxa of [Pleocyemata](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/NCBITaxon_6692), so the correct way to state that an anatomical entity only exists in [Astacidea](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/NCBITaxon_6712) and [Brachyura](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/NCBITaxon_6752) is

> '[in taxon](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002162)' some [Pleocyemata](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/NCBITaxon_6692)

---
Uberon contributors: Please ensure you read and understand the [guide to taxon constraints](https://oboacademy.github.io/obook/explanation/taxon-constraints-explainer) before adding/editing assertions that involve taxon constraints (any assertion that uses one of '[in taxon](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002162)', '[never in taxon](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002161)', [present in taxon](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002175)').

name: aorta-gonad-mesonephros
def: "A transient organ in the developing embryo composed of the dorsal aorta, developing gonads, and embryonic kidney. This structure is located in the embryonic mesoderm and serves as the primary site for the generation of definitive hematopoietic stem cells." [PMID:25686881, PMID:25749005, PMID:31813624]

[Term]
id: UBERON:8700001
name: follicle associated epithelium
def: "A specialized epithelial layer overlying the luminal surface of lymphoid follicles in gut-associated lymphoid tissue. It is found in Peyer's patches of the small intestine and appendix in humans and in the intestine and caecal patches in mice. This layer contains specialised M cells and has fewer goblet cells compared to surrounding tissue. This layer plays a crucial role in mucosal immune surveillance." [DOI:10.1007/s00702-022-02561-x, DOI:10.4061/2010/823710]
synonym: "FAE" EXACT [DOI:10.1007/s00702-022-02561-x]

[Term]
id: UBERON:8700002
name: stratum spinosum of esophageal epithelium
def: "The second deepest layer of the stratified squamous epithelium lining the esophagus, positioned directly above the stratum basale. It consists of multiple layers of keratinocytes connected by strong intercellular junctions called desmosomes, giving the cells a characteristic \"spiny\" appearance. This layer provides structural support, contributes to the epithelial barrier function, and serves as a transitional zone for keratinocytes migrating towards the luminal surface, playing a crucial role in maintaining the integrity and protective properties of the esophageal lining." [PMID:21126700, PMID:26019440]
is_a: UBERON:0010304 ! non-keratinized stratified squamous epithelium

[Term]
id: UBERON:8910001
name: stomatogastric ganglion
def: "The stomatogastric ganglion (STG) consists of about 30 neurons that form two central pattern generator circuits in crustaceans. STG neurons have large soma (~50-100 um) and have complex branches. The STG contains the motor neurons that innervate the striated muscles that move the gastric mill and pyloric regions of the stomach." [PMID: 17009928]

content.text:
I've identified the issue. There are 15 terms (UBERON:8910001 and UBERON:8910010 through UBERON:8910023) with both `in_taxon Astacidea` and `in_taxon Brachyura`, which creates contradicting taxon constraints. The fix is to replace these with a single `in_taxon Pleocyemata` (NCBITaxon:6692), which is the closest parent encompassing both.

Let me verify Pleocyemata is the correct common ancestor before making changes.

# Fix taxon constraints on stomatogastric nervous system terms (issue #3454)

## Summary

The 15 terms introduced in PR #3445 for the stomatogastric nervous system
each carried two separate `in_taxon` assertions:

```
relationship: in_taxon NCBITaxon:6712 ! Astacidea
relationship: in_taxon NCBITaxon:6752 ! Brachyura
```

As explained in issue #3454, this is invalid. The `in_taxon` (RO:0002162)
relation means "X is specific to taxon Y" — i.e. X cannot exist in any
other taxon that is not Y or a descendant of Y. Having two `in_taxon`
assertions to non-overlapping taxa is therefore contradictory.

The correct way to state that an anatomical entity only exists in
Astacidea and Brachyura, exclusive of all other taxa, is to use a
single `in_taxon` assertion to their closest common parent. Both
Astacidea and Brachyura are subtaxa of Pleocyemata (NCBITaxon:6692),
so each affected term now uses:

```
relationship: in_taxon NCBITaxon:6692 ! Pleocyemata
```

## Affected terms (15)

| ID | Name |
|----|------|
| UBERON:8910001 | stomatogastric ganglion |
| UBERON:8910010 | stomatogastric nerve (sensu Cancer borealis) |
| UBERON:8910011 | dorsal gastric nerve (sensu Cancer borealis) |
| UBERON:8910012 | gastropyloric nerve (sensu Cancer borealis) |
| UBERON:8910013 | lateral gastric nerve (sensu Cancer borealis) |
| UBERON:8910014 | lateral ventricular nerve (sensu Cancer borealis) |
| UBERON:8910015 | pyloric nerve (sensu Cancer borealis) |
| UBERON:8910016 | pyloric dilator nerve (sensu Cancer borealis) |
| UBERON:8910017 | inferior esophageal nerve (sensu Cancer borealis) |
| UBERON:8910018 | superior esophageal nerve (sensu Cancer borealis) |
| UBERON:8910019 | esophageal nerve (sensu Cancer borealis) |
| UBERON:8910020 | esophageal ganglion (sensu Cancer borealis) |
| UBERON:8910021 | commissural ganglion (sensu Cancer borealis) |
| UBERON:8910022 | junction of superior esophageal nerve and stomatogastric nerve (sensu Cancer borealis) |
| UBERON:8910023 | median ventricular nerve (sensu Cancer borealis) |

Note: UBERON:8910000 (stomatogastric nervous system) was *not* affected —
it already had a single, broader `in_taxon` assertion to Arthropoda
(NCBITaxon:6656), which is correct and was left unchanged.

## Process / checklist

- [x] Identified the 15 affected terms by grepping for the duplicate
      `in_taxon NCBITaxon:6712` / `in_taxon NCBITaxon:6752` pattern in
      `src/ontology/uberon-edit.obo`.
- [x] Verified that NCBITaxon:6692 (Pleocyemata) is the closest common
      parent of Astacidea (NCBITaxon:6712) and Brachyura (NCBITaxon:6752),
      as explicitly suggested by the issue author.
- [x] Used `obo-checkout.pl` to extract the 15 stanzas into individual
      files under `terms/`, edited each to replace the two `in_taxon`
      lines with one `in_taxon NCBITaxon:6692 ! Pleocyemata` line, and
      used `obo-checkin.pl` to write the changes back into
      `src/ontology/uberon-edit.obo`.
- [x] Verified post-change that the file contains 0 occurrences of
      `NCBITaxon:6712` and `NCBITaxon:6752`, and 15 occurrences of
      `NCBITaxon:6692`.
- [x] Reviewed the resulting `git diff`: 15 insertions, 30 deletions,
      no other changes — the edit is clean.

## Notes

- `robot convert` was not available in this environment, so the file
  was not reserialised through robot. The diff is small and clean
  (15 stanzas, each losing two lines and gaining one), so this should
  not be an issue, but a reserialisation pass can be run before final
  merge if the maintainers prefer.
- No other axioms (text definitions, synonyms, `is_a`, `part_of`,
  `overlaps`, contributor/date metadata) were touched. Only the
  contradictory `in_taxon` axioms were replaced.

Signed: @dragon-ai-agent
